skill.name: video-outpainting
skill.description: Guide agents to extend video canvas area with RunComfy, using Wan 2-7 edit-video for prompt-based aspect ratio changes and ComfyUI workflows when seam quality matters.
skill.summary: Extend or uncrop video frames with RunComfy while preserving the main action.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, and weak-crypto indicators in SKILL.md. Review shows the command and network usage is expected for a RunComfy CLI skill, the filesystem access documents local token storage, and the weak-crypto hits are markdown false positives. The remaining risk is medium because the skill instructs agents to run an external CLI that uploads or references user media through RunComfy cloud services.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Executes Cloud Video Jobs
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to use runcomfy CLI commands that submit video outpainting jobs and download outputs. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it depends on an installed external CLI and remote RunComfy services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains explicit runcomfy command examples and explains that the CLI posts to the Model API. The commands are scoped to RunComfy and no arbitrary shell construction is shown.

medium_findings.title: User Media Is Sent To External RunComfy Endpoints
medium_findings.description: The workflow requires video URLs or media job inputs to be processed by RunComfy cloud endpoints. Users should only submit media they are allowed to process with a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill documents a video_url input and states that the CLI posts to the Model API. The outbound endpoint allowlist reduces ambiguity, but third-party media processing remains a real privacy consideration.

medium_findings.title: Local API Token Storage Is Documented
medium_findings.description: The skill states that runcomfy login writes an API token under the user configuration directory and recommends RUNCOMFY_TOKEN for CI. This is expected behavior, but credentials require normal secret-handling care.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and file mode are explicitly documented. This is not exfiltration, but it confirms credential material can exist on disk during normal use.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Documentation And Product URLs
low_findings.description: The static network findings are mostly links to RunComfy, skills.sh, and documentation pages. These are expected marketplace and product references rather than hidden network behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are visible markdown links and are consistent with the skill description. No covert beaconing or unrelated external domain was found.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Scanner Hits Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak-cryptography findings occur on descriptive markdown lines, prompts, headings, and tables. No cryptographic implementation or weak algorithm use was found in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Inspection found prose and markdown structure at the reported lines, not hashing, encryption, or password logic. The scanner appears to have matched incidental text patterns.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Finding Is Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The reported reconnaissance line is an instruction about when to avoid the default model route for strict seam quality. No host inspection, environment discovery, or system enumeration was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is product guidance about video quality, not a command or reconnaissance workflow. No evidence found for system reconnaissance behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Scope Allows RunComfy Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed around Bash access for runcomfy commands. The scope is narrow, but execution still depends on the local CLI, account state, and user-provided job inputs.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools header and security section both limit shell use to runcomfy. This lowers command-injection risk while confirming external command execution is intentional.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party Source Video URLs Are Treated As Untrusted
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill warns that source video URLs are untrusted and should come from the user. This is good guidance, but it confirms the workflow can consume third-party media references.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The warning is explicit and mitigative. The risk remains because remote video inputs may contain misleading content or reference sensitive material.

content.value_statement: Video teams often need to convert clips across social, web, and ad formats without losing the main action. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to use RunComfy video outpainting routes for spatial canvas expansion.
actual_capabilities: Routes prompt-based video canvas expansion to Wan 2-7 edit-video through RunComfy.
Provides examples for changing vertical clips to horizontal formats.
Explains when to use dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows for better seams.
Gives prompt guidance for preserving central action and subject framing.
Documents common video outpainting scenarios and expected exit codes.
States security boundaries for CLI use, token storage, and outbound endpoints.
limitations: It depends on the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy account or token.
Wan 2-7 edit-video may show seam or consistency issues on hero-quality work.
ComfyUI outpainting workflows are GUI workflows, not direct CLI endpoints.
It does not perform still image outpainting, video inpainting, or temporal video extension.

use_cases.title: Convert social clips for YouTube
use_cases.description: Turn a vertical short-form clip into a horizontal version while keeping the subject centered.

use_cases.title: Prepare campaign video variants
use_cases.description: Create aspect ratio variants for ads, banners, and landing pages from one source clip.

use_cases.title: Choose the right outpainting route
use_cases.description: Decide between quick CLI-based edits and higher-quality ComfyUI workflows for production delivery.

prompt_templates.title: Basic canvas extension
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple aspect ratio change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extend this video to a 16:9 horizontal frame. Keep the subject centered and continue the same background, lighting, and camera distance.

prompt_templates.title: Social format conversion
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a reusable version for another platform.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this vertical social video for YouTube. Add matching environment on the left and right sides, and preserve the original action.

prompt_templates.title: Quality-sensitive outpaint plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare quick and high-quality routes before running work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this video outpainting request and recommend the RunComfy route. Explain whether Wan 2-7 edit-video is enough or a ComfyUI workflow is better.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-pass expansion strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow for larger canvas changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a two-pass video outpainting workflow for this clip. Limit each pass, preserve central framing, and note quality risks before running it.

output_examples.input: A user asks to turn a vertical product demo into a horizontal website hero video.
output_examples.output: The assistant recommends a RunComfy edit-video route, drafts a preservation-focused prompt, and notes when a ComfyUI workflow is better for seam quality.

output_examples.input: A user wants to expand old square footage into a modern widescreen frame.
output_examples.output: The assistant explains that quick prompt-based editing may work for drafts, but a dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflow is safer for production.

output_examples.input: A user provides a clip and asks for a larger frame without changing the subject.
output_examples.output: The assistant focuses the prompt on canvas size, matching background details, stable lighting, and preservation of the central action.

best_practices: Use only video URLs or media files that the user explicitly provided for this task.
Lead prompts with the target canvas change, then describe background and lighting continuity.
Use dedicated ComfyUI outpainting workflows when seam quality or temporal consistency is critical.
anti_patterns: Do not submit private or licensed media to RunComfy without permission.
Do not use repeated outpainting passes for large expansions without checking quality after each pass.
Do not treat video outpainting as temporal extension or mask-based inpainting.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents plan and run video canvas expansion with RunComfy for aspect ratio changes and uncropping.

faq.question: Does it generate extra time in a video?
faq.answer: No. It expands the spatial frame. It does not add new frames before or after the clip.

faq.question: Can it convert vertical video to horizontal video?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes guidance for extending the left and right sides while preserving the centered subject.

faq.question: When should I use a ComfyUI workflow?
faq.answer: Use ComfyUI when production quality, seam handling, or temporal consistency is more important than a quick draft.

faq.question: What accounts or tools are required?
faq.answer: You need the RunComfy CLI and a RunComfy login or token before running cloud video jobs.

faq.question: Is there a privacy concern?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow sends or references media through RunComfy endpoints, so only use media approved for third-party processing.
